steps.title: Set up the cost formula
steps.content:
The first day costs \$54, and each additional day costs \$27. For $d$ days, there is 1 first day and $(d-1)$ additional days, so the total cost is:
$$C(d) = 54 + 27(d-1)$$

steps.title: Expand and simplify to match a choice
steps.content:
Distribute the 27:
$$C(d) = 54 + 27d - 27$$
Combine like terms:
$$C(d) = 27d + 27$$
This confirms A is correct.
